ops.text:
Lane E on task d5cfd966 (instrument card headline defect), referencing E17 log 67cbc63d and the E17-rep artifacts (~/.cc-steer/experiments/E17-rep/out).

DIAGNOSIS (from read of deployed prompt + E17/E17-rep raw votes): the deployed pairwise panel judge (cc_steer/retrain/judged.py::panel_labels, PROMPT_VERSION watcher-fire-harm-v1) collapsed to STAY_SILENT (E17: 30/30 loss; E17-rep: 28/30 loss, J=0.00 both passes, pairwise test-retest kappa 0.0). Three prompt asymmetries drive the collapse: (1) v1's selection criterion literally instructs the bias — "pick the one whose choice is the more appropriate, helpful, and least disruptive call": silence is definitionally least disruptive, so the tie-breaker always points at [STAY_SILENT]; (2) v1 never defines when [STEER] is the right call, so the judge falls back on LLM conservatism about interrupting an engineer; (3) the [STEER] side renders as a bare action tag with no note text, making the interrupt side look contentless against an equally bare but "honest" silence. The verdict schema (Pairwise.winner only, no rationale field) is athome-owned and unchanged.

CANDIDATE FIX (c1, staged in workspace cc-steer-we): (a) build_prompt v2 — PROMPT_VERSION watcher-fire-harm-v2 — drops "least disruptive", defines both actions symmetrically using the golden README's own warranted definition ("a supervisor watching in real time should have intervened at this point ... independent of what happened next" / "on a reasonable track, or nothing yet to act on"), states the two mistakes cost the same and forbids silence-as-safe-default, and instructs that only the action tag is shown so the choice, not note wording, is judged; (b) decision structure — panel_labels now takes PANEL_VOTES=3 position-debiased votes per row on consecutive seeds (1729/1730/1731) and majority-maps wins>losses (ties abstain) to fire-warranted, matching the calibration instrument exactly. Partial prompt edit was staged by a prior dead lane; audited and kept as-is, K=3 structure added by this lane.

PRE-REGISTERED CALIBRATION PROTOCOL (before any voting): packet = _base-20260719 snapshot golden watcher-fires, 30 rows, gold = manifest design-intent strata (15 warranted / 15 negative; labels.json still absent — same gold caveat as E17-rep). Votes: pairwise_vote, backend=claude tier=large (opus), PanelGrant, cache=None, behind eval "$(ccp env)". K=3 votes/row (seeds 1729,1730,1731); per-row pred_warranted = wins > losses. TWO independent identical runs (fresh calls, no cache) for test-retest. Metrics: per-run TPR/FPR/J; Newcombe 95% CI on J (Wilson method 10); Cohen kappa between the two runs' 30 per-row majority labels; pooled 2-pass rates. A 6-row probe (3 warranted / 3 negative, 1 vote each) runs first as a collapse check and is recorded here, not cherry-picked.

PRE-REGISTERED SHIP GATE: J point estimate >= 0.3 with TPR > FPR in BOTH runs, AND run-to-run kappa >= 0.29 (no worse than the E17 pointwise reference). Gate fails -> fix stays unshipped, task d5cfd966 stays open, full calibration table returned. Every candidate's numbers get appended here — no silent iteration. Spend cap $10 opus total (full 2-run calibration est. ~180 votes x ~3.8K input tok = ~$4-5).

Note (outside this gate, recorded for honesty): the deployed prove_gate acceptance in the packet README is >= 24/30 agreement with human labels + non-constant decider. Against strata-gold, J = 0.3 at FPR ~0.07 corresponds to ~20/30 agreement — clearing this lane's gate does NOT yet imply the deployment agreement gate passes once human labels land.
